title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year contract with the Cincinnati Bengals, making them his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger played nine games with the Steelers last season, recording 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack.
Dugger, a former Patriots captain, had a notable season in 2023 with 109 tackles and seven pass breakups.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes returned from vacation to find his truck stolen from Logan Airport’s central parking garage.
Similar thefts at Logan Airport have occurred, with stolen vehicles reportedly ending up in New Jersey.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating, but no arrests have been made regarding the recent vehicle thefts.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a registered nurse from Wakefield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto works with students with complex medical needs, inspiring her to advocate for inclusion and empowerment through running.
Special Olympics Massachusetts focuses on athletes' abilities, aligning with Peduto's values of empowerment beyond diagnoses.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover Police Department investigates unauthorized Facebook comment by former employee about ex-officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons's civil litigation.
Former officer Fitzsimmons plans civil suit after being shot by a colleague; she was found not guilty of assault last week.
NAPD and town officials reviewing social media policies, auditing accounts to prevent unauthorized access by former employees.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a Waltham seafood and steak restaurant, has closed after seven years due to rising costs.
Located at 74 Tower Rd., the restaurant cited economic conditions as a reason for shutting down.
The Federal's sister property, Stazione di Federal, will honor all outstanding gift cards.

title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com has curated a list of 16 new books for spring, organized on a grid for easy selection.
The reading guide categorizes books as fast-paced or slow-burning, and light to dark, to suit different moods.
Readers can sign up for Boston.com's spring book matrix to receive detailed recommendations and top four picks.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express will open a two-story Centurion Lounge in Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
The lounge will feature Boston-inspired design, local artwork, and Logan's first open-air terrace.
Massport approved up to $75 million for construction, while American Express committed $391 million for the project.

title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's at 96 Brookline Ave. near Fenway Park has closed after 8 years, announced via Facebook.
Opened in 2018 in the former Tilted Kilt space, it was a popular spot for pre-game gatherings.
West End Johnnie's, the original location, remains open at 138 Portland St., Boston.

title: Jayson Tatum reacts to Duke’s buzzer-beater loss to UConn: ‘I was sick’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum, former Duke player, expressed disappointment after Duke lost to UConn in the Elite Eight via a buzzer-beater.
Tatum, recently named Duke's chief basketball officer, advised Duke players and coaches throughout the season.
UConn's last-second victory places them in the Final Four, pursuing a third national title in four years.

title: Boston man charged with sexually assaulting MBTA bus driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Vandenbroeke, 41, charged after allegedly groping an MBTA bus driver on a Silver Line bus in Boston.
Vandenbroeke has a history of violent offenses and was held on $500 bail, pleading not guilty to multiple charges.
Incident was recorded on bus surveillance; Vandenbroeke arrested at Nubian Square station and due in court April 21.
